purpose: Define when Geode review outputs are research-only, when they are ready for internal review, and when they may support externally relied-on guidance.

reviewer_roles.label: Data Reviewer
reviewer_roles.description: Reviews source fidelity, extraction quality, and packet completeness.

reviewer_roles.label: Corpus Maintainer
reviewer_roles.description: Applies reviewed canonical changes through guarded commands only.

reviewer_roles.label: Legal Reviewer
reviewer_roles.description: Approves whether reviewed outputs may support externally relied-on guidance.

approval_criteria.label: Source fidelity
approval_criteria.description: Every claim must be traceable to the cited source sentence or document.

approval_criteria.label: Canonical validation
approval_criteria.description: Any canonical change must validate against the rule-unit schema.

approval_criteria.label: Review decision logged
approval_criteria.description: A packet must have an append-only review decision before canonical apply.

approval_criteria.label: Legal reviewer approval
approval_criteria.description: Externally relied-on guidance requires explicit legal reviewer approval.

reliance_boundaries.boundary: Review packets organize extraction review. They are not legal advice and do not change canonical law.
Compliance paths are source-backed review workflows and do not replace legal review.
Canonical rule units are structured extraction records. External reliance still requires legal reviewer approval.

external_use_limits: Do not present Geode outputs as legal advice.
Do not externally rely on pending review packets.
Do not apply canonical changes without validated review decisions.
Do not treat popularity, score, or confidence as legal authority.
Do not remove the source citation or reliance boundary from exported guidance.
canonical_change_rules: Canonical changes must use the guarded apply path.
The prior canonical file must be snapshotted before replacement.
Replacement rule units must pass schema validation before apply.
Approve-only decisions do not rewrite canonical rule units.
